note: Sprint 5.22: Operator Loop From Existing Request v0.1. scripts/trinity/useful_compute_operator_loop.py gains --request-json <path> (mutually exclusive with --input-bundle). When supplied, the loop validates the file against trinity-useful-compute-request/v0.1 (schema + request_id pattern + input_bundle_sha256), canonically re-emits it to <out-dir>/request.json, marks task_builder as completed up-front, records request_source=existing_request + source_request_sha256 (sha256 of source bytes) + source_request_path_basename (basename only — no absolute paths) in operator_run.json, and continues from worker. Downstream steps see the exact same file layout as in built mode. Schema useful-compute-operator-run/v0.1 gains three new required fields: request_source (enum built|existing_request), source_request_sha256 (oneOf 64-hex/null), source_request_path_basename (oneOf string-max-256/null). In built mode the new fields are null. operator_run_id hash now includes request_source + source_request_sha256, so a built run and an existing-request run with otherwise identical operator inputs produce different operator_run_ids — they are distinct lineages. Resume re-verifies the imported request's sha256 against the recorded value; tampered run/request.json aborts with rc=2. Pipeline now wired end-to-end across Sprints 5.20 → 5.21 → 5.22: scientific_prompt_intake.py → useful_compute_task_builder.py --from-scientific-intake → useful_compute_operator_loop.py --request-json. Tests: 17 new functional tests (request-json happy path, manifest layout, no absolute path leak, mutual exclusion, invalid JSON / wrong schema / bad request_id / missing file rejected, resume idempotence, resume tamper detection, built-mode regression, schema validation, operator_run_id distinct between modes), 3 new schema tests, all 13 static-safety tests stay green. No src/, no consensus, no RPC, no wallet, no signer, no wire-format change. No subprocess. No --real-sign / human-broadcast / sost-cli reference.
